---
title: "Nick Anderson"
output: html_document
theme: null
---

Welcome to my research hosting site. I am an economics PhD candidate at The New School for Social Research.

# **Welcome, here are some projects of mine**

## Projects

### [Research](research.html)

My empirical work on public finance and data.

### [Heterodata](https://heterodata.org)

The research-data platform: a hub of data sites including **Gerhard** (US public finance, [gerhard.heterodata.org](https://gerhard.heterodata.org)), **Shaikh Data** ([shaikh.heterodata.org](https://shaikh.heterodata.org) — replications of Anwar Shaikh's *Capitalism* and *Measuring the Wealth of Nations*), **Lewis** (international economics, [lewis.heterodata.org](https://lewis.heterodata.org)), **Leonid** (Soviet statistics, [leonid.heterodata.org](https://leonid.heterodata.org)), and **Leontief** (US input–output, [leontief.heterodata.org](https://leontief.heterodata.org)), plus affiliated standalone sites: [volcker.org](https://volcker.org) (banking analysis), [freenic.org](https://freenic.org) (banking data), [foodberg.org](https://foodberg.org) (food prices), and [methodex.fyi](https://methodex.fyi) (statistical methodology).

### [Economics at the New School](tnse.html)

A three-part institutional and intellectual history of The New School's economics department. A 121-page draft of Part 1 is available [on Google Drive](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/17Ip5x5DqTrQo8c7PagMYfp7mKX2KJDvg?usp=sharing).

### [AI Tools](aitools.html)

The open-source research tooling I built for AI-assisted empirical economics, with links to the GitHub repositories.

### HMDA Explore

An interactive explorer over Home Mortgage Disclosure Act data, in two versions side by side:

- [andenick.shinyapps.io/HMDAExplore](https://andenick.shinyapps.io/HMDAExplore/) — the earlier interactive draft (R-Shiny; slower to start).
- [hmdaexplore.com](https://hmdaexplore.com) — the actively developed web version.

---

Thank you and let me know if you have any questions!

Nick

---

Last compiled: `r Sys.Date()`
